# tmux-lifecycle: verify that af suspend/resume drives the tmux seam correctly.
#
# Uses a state-bearing fake tmux (at $WORK/mytmux/tmux) that maintains a
# session list in $WORK/tmux-sessions.txt. A session "exists" iff its name
# appears in that file. The fake handles has-session, new-session, kill-session,
# and set-option; all other sub-commands echo their args and exit 0.
#
# Three scenarios are exercised:
#   1. suspend — state.toml transitions to suspended; tmux is NOT contacted.
#   2. resume (non-bare) — has-session returns miss → new-session is called.
#   3. resume --bare — tmux seam is bypassed entirely.

env HOME=$WORK/home

# Activate the per-test smart fake ahead of the global passthrough fake.
chmod 755 mytmux/tmux
env PATH=$WORK/mytmux:$PATH

# ── Scenario 1: suspend ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# State file starts as "active". After suspend it must say "suspended".
# Crucially, the fake tmux must NOT be consulted (sessions file stays absent).

exec af suspend demo
stdout 'workstream demo -> suspended'

! exists tmux-sessions.txt
grep 'status = "suspended"' $HOME/.local/share/af/v1/sessions/demo/state.toml

# ── Scenario 2: non-bare resume ───────────────────────────────────────────────
# Session "af-demo" is absent from the fake (sessions file doesn't exist yet).
# ResumeWorkstream must call has-session (miss → exit 1), then new-session,
# which appends "af-demo" to the file.

exec af resume demo
stdout 'workstream demo -> active'

exists tmux-sessions.txt
grep 'af-demo' tmux-sessions.txt
grep 'status = "active"' $HOME/.local/share/af/v1/sessions/demo/state.toml

# ── Scenario 3: bare resume skips the tmux seam ───────────────────────────────
# Suspend again, delete the sessions file to give a clean baseline, then
# resume with --bare. The file must remain absent: bare mode never calls tmux.

exec af suspend demo
stdout 'workstream demo -> suspended'

rm tmux-sessions.txt

exec af resume demo --bare
stdout 'workstream demo -> active'

! exists tmux-sessions.txt

# ── Initial file content ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- home/.local/share/af/v1/sessions/demo/state.toml --
schema_version = 1

[session]
id = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001"
name = "demo"
status = "active"
created_at = 2026-05-22T10:00:00Z

[worktree]
path = "/tmp/worktree-demo"
branch = "feat/demo"
base_branch = "main"

[execution]
tmux_session = "af-demo"

-- mytmux/tmux --
#!/bin/sh
# State-bearing fake tmux for testscript integration tests (ADR-040 + ADR-046).
#
# Active session names are stored one-per-line in $WORK/tmux-sessions.txt.
# Supported sub-commands:
#   has-session  -t NAME      → exit 0 if NAME in file, exit 1 otherwise
#   new-session  -d -s NAME -c PATH → append NAME to file, exit 0
#   kill-session -t NAME      → remove NAME from file, exit 0
#   set-option   ...          → exit 0 (marks @AF_SESSION; ignored here)
#   *                         → echo args, exit 0
SESSIONS="$WORK/tmux-sessions.txt"
subcmd="$1"
shift

case "$subcmd" in
  has-session)
    name=""
    while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
      case "$1" in
        -t) shift; name="$1" ;;
      esac
      shift
    done
    if [ -f "$SESSIONS" ] && grep -qxF "$name" "$SESSIONS"; then
      exit 0
    fi
    exit 1
    ;;
  new-session)
    name=""
    while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
      case "$1" in
        -s) shift; name="$1" ;;
        -c) shift ;;
      esac
      shift
    done
    echo "$name" >> "$SESSIONS"
    exit 0
    ;;
  kill-session)
    name=""
    while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
      case "$1" in
        -t) shift; name="$1" ;;
      esac
      shift
    done
    if [ -f "$SESSIONS" ]; then
      grep -vxF "$name" "$SESSIONS" > "$SESSIONS.tmp" 2>/dev/null || true
      mv "$SESSIONS.tmp" "$SESSIONS"
    fi
    exit 0
    ;;
  set-option)
    # Called by CreateSession to mark @AF_SESSION=1 — succeed silently.
    exit 0
    ;;
  *)
    echo "fake tmux $subcmd $*"
    exit 0
    ;;
esac
